stories.headline: Bitcoin Posts Best Month in a Year as $5B USDT Injection Pushes BTC Above $77K
stories.tldr: Bitcoin is up 13.6% in April and on pace for its strongest monthly close since early 2025, with Tether minting $5B in fresh USDT to refuel the rally.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Stablecoin supply expansion is the cleanest leading indicator of crypto liquidity, and Tether's market cap pushing toward $150B signals risk appetite is back before the Fed meeting.
the_details: BTC held above $77,000 Friday, up 13.6% in April, with the next test the upcoming Fed decision that could flip $79K resistance into support.
USDT supply has grown roughly $5 billion in April toward an all-time high near $150 billion, fueling spot demand across exchanges.
Bitcoin's 30-day correlation with the U.S. Dollar Index has fallen to -0.90, the deepest inverse reading since 2022.

stories.headline: Solana Foundation Steps In With USDT Loan to Aave as $13B DeFi Liquidity Hole Persists
stories.tldr: The Solana Foundation extended an emergency USDT loan to Aave to backstop liquidity after the Kelp exploit triggered $13B in DeFi outflows.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A layer-1 foundation propping up a competing chain's lender is a sign that DeFi's contagion risk has crossed ecosystem lines — and that survival now beats tribal turf wars.
the_details: Aave saw $8.45 billion in deposits exit over 48 hours after attackers used stolen rsETH from the April 18 KelpDAO exploit as collateral.
Total DeFi TVL slid from $99.5B to $86.3B in two days, a $13.21 billion contraction across protocols.
Solana Foundation's USDT injection is its first publicly disclosed cross-chain liquidity backstop, per Crypto Briefing.

stories.headline: Bitcoin ETFs Absorb $2B in 8 Days as Institutional Bid Extends Inflow Streak
stories.tldr: Spot bitcoin ETFs pulled in $2 billion across an 8-day window through April 24, the longest sustained inflow streak since the post-halving rally.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Persistent ETF demand at $77K shows allocators are buying the rebound, not fading it — a structural bid that historically tightens float and amplifies upside on macro tailwinds.
the_details: Cumulative ETF inflows hit $2 billion over 8 trading days ending April 24, per CoinDesk daily flow data.
BlackRock's IBIT options open interest surpassed Deribit on Friday, marking U.S. regulated venues eclipsing offshore for the first time.
Inflows came despite Middle East tensions and elevated oil prices, suggesting institutional buyers are looking through near-term macro noise.

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley Launches $1 Stablecoin Fund, First Bulge-Bracket Bank to Issue Onchain Cash
stories.tldr: Morgan Stanley debuted a $1-pegged tokenized stablecoin fund on April 24, becoming the first bulge-bracket U.S. bank to ship a yield-bearing onchain dollar product.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: With the GENIUS Act framework live, Wall Street stablecoins are no longer a thought experiment — Morgan Stanley moving first hands JPMorgan and Goldman a clock to match or cede the rails.
the_details: Fund launched April 24, 2026, structured under the GENIUS Act's payment stablecoin issuer framework finalized this month.
Targets institutional treasury and brokerage clients, distributing yield from short-duration Treasuries via tokenized shares.
FDIC issued proposed rulemaking April 2026 setting capital and reserve standards for FDIC-supervised stablecoin issuers.

stories.headline: IRS Form 1099-DA Goes Live, Forcing Crypto Brokers Into Mandatory Cost-Basis Reporting
stories.tldr: The IRS formally activated mandatory cost-basis reporting for digital asset brokers via Form 1099-DA on April 15, ending years of voluntary compliance.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Onchain anonymity at U.S. exchanges is now a reporting fiction — every Coinbase, Kraken, and Robinhood trade flows directly to the IRS with cost basis attached, closing the favorite loophole of retail traders.
the_details: Effective April 15, 2026, brokers must transmit cost basis on every digital asset disposal via Form 1099-DA.
Rule implements Section 80603 of the 2021 Infrastructure Act, delayed multiple times before this month's enforcement deadline.
Decentralized front-ends got a separate reprieve April 13 when SEC staff said they don't need broker registration under specified conditions.
